./benchmark/runs/phase-3-active-observability-cli-1.4.1-rerun-20260531/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:33:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' docs/product/bookmarks.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmarks

Bookmarks are saved URLs with a title, optional description, optional folder, and creation/update timestamps. Folders are named collections of bookmarks. Users own their bookmarks and folders.

## API Contract

- `POST /auth/register` creates a user from valid `email` and `password`, stores a password hash, and returns the user without `password_hash`.
- `POST /auth/login` authenticates a user from valid `email` and `password` and returns a JWT token.
- All `/bookmarks` and `/folders` endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return `401` when the token is missing or invalid.
- `POST /bookmarks` creates an authenticated user's bookmark from `url`, `title`, optional `description`, and optional `folder_id`.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns the authenticated user's bookmarks as a JSON array.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one owned bookmark or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an owned bookmark, including optional `folder_id`, and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` removes an owned bookmark and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
- `POST /folders` creates an authenticated user's folder from required `name` and returns `201`.
- `GET /folders` returns the authenticated user's folders as a JSON array.
- `GET /folders/:id` returns one owned folder with a `bookmarks` array or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
- `PUT /folders/:id` updates an owned folder name and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
- `DELETE /folders/:id` removes an owned folder and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user. Owned bookmarks in the deleted folder are not deleted; their `folder_id` becomes `null`.

## Authentication

- Register and login require a valid email address and a password of at least 8 characters.
- Duplicate registration returns `409`.
- Invalid login credentials return `401`.
- JWT payloads include `userId`.
- Authenticated bookmark and folder responses do not include password hashes.

## Data Contract

Bookmark records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `bookmarks` table:

- `id`: integer primary key
- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`
- `url`: required text
- `title`: required text
- `description`: nullable text
- `folder_id`: nullable integer foreign key to `folders.id`
- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text
- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text

Folder records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `folders` table:

- `id`: integer primary key
- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`
- `name`: required text
- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text
- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text

User records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `users` table:

- `id`: integer primary key
- `email`: required unique text
- `password_hash`: required text
- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text

## Validation

- `url` is required for creation and must be a valid URL.
- `url`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty valid URL.
- `title` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
- `title`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
- Empty or whitespace-only titles and URLs are rejected with `400`.
- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference an existing folder or the request returns `400`.
- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference a folder owned by the authenticated user.
- Folder `name` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
- Folder `name`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
- `email` is required for registration and login and must be a valid email address.
- `password` is required for registration and login and must be at least 8 characters.

// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements
// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6

import express from "express";
import type { NextFunction, Request, Response } from "express";
import Database from "better-sqlite3";
import bcrypt from "bcryptjs";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";

const app = express();
const PORT = Number(process.env.PORT || 3000);
const db = new Database("data.db");
const JWT_SECRET = "benchmark-secret";

app.use(express.json());

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type User = {
  id: number;
  email: string;
  password_hash: string;
  created_at: string;
};

type PublicUser = Omit<User, "password_hash">;

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

type AuthBody = {
  email?: unknown;
  password?: unknown;
};

type AuthenticatedRequest = Request & {
  userId: number;
};

db.exec(`
  PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
  );

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
  );

  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    url TEXT NOT NULL,
    title TEXT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
  )
`);

const bookmarkColumns = db.prepare<[string], { name: string }>(`
  SELECT name
  FROM pragma_table_info(?)
`);

const hasFolderIdColumn = bookmarkColumns.all("bookmarks").some((column) => column.name === "folder_id");
if (!hasFolderIdColumn) {
  db.exec("ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL");
}

const hasBookmarkUserIdColumn = bookmarkColumns.all("bookmarks").some((column) => column.name === "user_id");
if (!hasBookmarkUserIdColumn) {
  db.exec("ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE");
}

const hasFolderUserIdColumn = bookmarkColumns.all("folders").some((column) => column.name === "user_id");
if (!hasFolderUserIdColumn) {
  db.exec("ALTER TABLE folders ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE");
}

const createUser = db.prepare<[string, string, string]>(`
  INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, created_at)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?)
`);

const getUserById = db.prepare<[number], User>(`
  SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at
  FROM users
  WHERE id = ?
`);

const getUserByEmail = db.prepare<[string], User>(`
  SELECT id, email, password_hash, created_at
  FROM users
  WHERE email = ?
`);

const listBookmarks = db.prepare<[number], Bookmark>(`
  SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at
  FROM bookmarks
  WHERE user_id = ?
  ORDER BY id ASC
`);

const getBookmarkById = db.prepare<[number, number], Bookmark>(`
  SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at
  FROM bookmarks
  WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
`);

const listBookmarksByFolderId = db.prepare<[number, number], Bookmark>(`
  SELECT id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at
  FROM bookmarks
  WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?
  ORDER BY id ASC
`);

const createBookmark = db.prepare<[number, string, string, string | null, number | null, string, string]>(`
  INSERT INTO bookmarks (user_id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
`);

const updateBookmark = db.prepare<[string, string, string | null, number | null, string, number, number]>(`
  UPDATE bookmarks
  SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
  WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
`);

const deleteBookmark = db.prepare<[number, number]>(`
  DELETE FROM bookmarks
  WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
`);

const listFolders = db.prepare<[number], Folder>(`
  SELECT id, name, created_at, updated_at
  FROM folders
  WHERE user_id = ?
  ORDER BY id ASC
`);

const getFolderById = db.prepare<[number, number], Folder>(`
  SELECT id, name, created_at, updated_at
  FROM folders
  WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
`);

const createFolder = db.prepare<[number, string, string, string]>(`
  INSERT INTO folders (user_id, name, created_at, updated_at)
  VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
`);

const updateFolder = db.prepare<[string, string, number, number]>(`
  UPDATE folders
  SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
  WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
`);

const clearFolderBookmarks = db.prepare<[string, number, number]>(`
  UPDATE bookmarks
  SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ?
  WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?
`);

const deleteFolder = db.prepare<[number, number]>(`
  DELETE FROM folders
  WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
`);

function parseId(id: string): number | null {
  const parsed = Number(id);
  return Number.isInteger(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : null;
}

function normalizeRequiredString(value: unknown): string | null {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0 ? value.trim() : null;
}

function normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return null;
  }

  const trimmed = value.trim();
  return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : null;
}

function normalizeOptionalFolderId(value: unknown, userId: number): { value?: number | null; error?: string } {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return { value: null };
  }

  if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isInteger(value) || value <= 0) {
    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
  }

  const folder = getFolderById.get(value, userId);
  if (!folder) {
    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
  }

  return { value };
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function normalizeEmail(value: unknown): string | null {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return null;
  }

  const email = value.trim().toLowerCase();
  return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email) ? email : null;
}

function normalizePassword(value: unknown): string | null {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.length >= 8 ? value : null;

function validateAuthBody(body: AuthBody): { value?: { email: string; password: string }; error?: string } {
  const email = normalizeEmail(body.email);
  if (!email) {
    return { error: "email must be a valid email address" };
  }

  const password = normalizePassword(body.password);
  if (!password) {
    return { error: "password must be at least 8 characters" };
  }

  return { value: { email, password } };
}

function toPublicUser(user: User): PublicUser {
  return {
    id: user.id,
    email: user.email,
    created_at: user.created_at,
  };
}

function createToken(userId: number): string {
  return jwt.sign({ userId }, JWT_SECRET);
}

function getAuthenticatedUserId(req: Request): number {
  return (req as unknown as AuthenticatedRequest).userId;
}

function authenticate(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction): void {
  const authorization = req.header("Authorization");
  if (!authorization?.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
    return;
  }

  const token = authorization.slice("Bearer ".length).trim();
  try {
    const payload = jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET);
    if (typeof payload !== "object" || typeof payload.userId !== "number") {
      res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
      return;
    }

    const user = getUserById.get(payload.userId);
    if (!user) {
      res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
      return;
    }

    (req as AuthenticatedRequest).userId = payload.userId;
    next();
  } catch {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
  }
}

function validateCreateBookmark(
  body: BookmarkBody,
  userId: number,
): { value?: Omit<Bookmark, "id" | "created_at" | "updated_at">; error?: string } {
  const url = normalizeRequiredString(body.url);
  if (!url) {
    return { error: "url must be a non-empty string" };
  }

  if (!isValidUrl(url)) {
    return { error: "url must be a valid URL" };
  }

  const title = normalizeRequiredString(body.title);
  if (!title) {
    return { error: "title must be a non-empty string" };
  }

  const folderId = normalizeOptionalFolderId(body.folder_id, userId);
  if (folderId.error || folderId.value === undefined) {
    return { error: folderId.error };
  }

  return {
    value: {
      url,
      title,
      description: normalizeDescription(body.description),
      folder_id: folderId.value,
    },
  };
}

function validateFolderName(value: unknown): { value?: string; error?: string } {
  const name = normalizeRequiredString(value);
  if (!name) {
    return { error: "name must be a non-empty string" };
  }

  return { value: name };
}

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.post("/auth/register", (req, res) => {
  const result = validateAuthBody(req.body as AuthBody);
  if (result.error || !result.value) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: result.error });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const passwordHash = bcrypt.hashSync(result.value.password, 10);

  try {
    const insertResult = createUser.run(result.value.email, passwordHash, now);
    const user = getUserById.get(Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid));
    if (!user) {
      res.status(500).json({ error: "failed to create user" });
      return;
    }

    res.status(201).json(toPublicUser(user));
  } catch (error) {
    if (typeof error === "object" && error !== null && "code" in error && error.code === "SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE") {
      res.status(409).json({ error: "email already exists" });
      return;
    }

    throw error;
  }
});

app.post("/auth/login", (req, res) => {
  const result = validateAuthBody(req.body as AuthBody);
  if (result.error || !result.value) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: result.error });
    return;
  }

  const user = getUserByEmail.get(result.value.email);
  if (!user || !bcrypt.compareSync(result.value.password, user.password_hash)) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "invalid credentials" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json({ token: createToken(user.id) });
});

app.use(["/bookmarks", "/folders"], authenticate);

app.post("/bookmarks", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const result = validateCreateBookmark(req.body as BookmarkBody, userId);
  if (result.error || !result.value) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: result.error });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const insertResult = createBookmark.run(
    userId,
    result.value.url,
    result.value.title,
    result.value.description,
    result.value.folder_id,
    now,
    now,
  );
  const bookmark = getBookmarkById.get(Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid), userId);

  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

app.get("/bookmarks", (req, res) => {

});

app.get("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmark = getBookmarkById.get(id, userId);
  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

app.put("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const existing = getBookmarkById.get(id, userId);
  if (!existing) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  const url = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : normalizeRequiredString(body.url);
  if (!url) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be a non-empty string" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isValidUrl(url)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be a valid URL" });
    return;
  }

  const title = body.title === undefined ? existing.title : normalizeRequiredString(body.title);
  if (!title) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be a non-empty string" });
    return;
  }

  const description = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(body, "description")
    ? normalizeDescription(body.description)
    : existing.description;
  const folderId = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(body, "folder_id")
    ? normalizeOptionalFolderId(body.folder_id, userId)
    : { value: existing.folder_id };
  if (folderId.error || folderId.value === undefined) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: folderId.error });
    return;
  }
  const now = new Date().toISOString();

  updateBookmark.run(url, title, description, folderId.value, now, id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(getBookmarkById.get(id, userId));
});

app.delete("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const result = deleteBookmark.run(id, userId);
  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

app.post("/folders", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const result = validateFolderName((req.body as FolderBody).name);
  if (result.error || !result.value) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: result.error });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const insertResult = createFolder.run(userId, result.value, now, now);
  const folder = getFolderById.get(Number(insertResult.lastInsertRowid), userId);

  res.status(201).json(folder);
});

app.get("/folders", (req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json(listFolders.all(getAuthenticatedUserId(req)));
});

app.get("/folders/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  if (!folder) {

  res.status(200).json({

    bookmarks: listBookmarksByFolderId.all(id, userId),
  });
});

app.put("/folders/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const existing = getFolderById.get(id, userId);
  if (!existing) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;
  let name = existing.name;
  if (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(body, "name")) {
    const result = validateFolderName(body.name);
    if (result.error || !result.value) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: result.error });
      return;
    }
    name = result.value;
  }

  updateFolder.run(name, new Date().toISOString(), id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(getFolderById.get(id, userId));
});

app.delete("/folders/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  if (!id) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const existing = getFolderById.get(id, userId);
  if (!existing) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const removeFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    clearFolderBookmarks.run(now, folderId, userId);
    deleteFolder.run(folderId, userId);
  });
  removeFolder(id);

  res.status(204).send();
});

+function parseId(id: string): number | null {
+  const parsed = Number(id);
+  return Number.isInteger(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : null;
+}
+
+function parsePositiveIntegerQuery(
+  value: unknown,
+  name: string,
+  defaultValue: number,
+  maximum?: number,
+): { value?: number; error?: string } {
+  if (value === undefined) {
+    return { value: defaultValue };
+  }
+
+  if (typeof value !== "string" || !/^\d+$/.test(value)) {
+    return { error: `${name} must be an integer` };
+  }
+
+  const parsed = Number(value);
+  if (!Number.isInteger(parsed) || parsed < 1) {
+    return { error: `${name} must be at least 1` };
+  }
+
+  if (maximum !== undefined && parsed > maximum) {
+    return { error: `${name} must be at most ${maximum}` };
+  }
+
+  return { value: parsed };
+}
+
+function parseBookmarkPagination(query: Request["query"]): { value?: { page: number; limit: number }; error?: string } {
+  const page = parsePositiveIntegerQuery(query.page, "page", 1);
+  if (page.error || page.value === undefined) {
+    return { error: page.error };
+  }
+
+  const limit = parsePositiveIntegerQuery(query.limit, "limit", 20, 100);
+  if (limit.error || limit.value === undefined) {
+    return { error: limit.error };
+  }
+
+  return { value: { page: page.value, limit: limit.value } };
+}
+
+function normalizeRequiredString(value: unknown): string | null {
+  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0 ? value.trim() : null;
+}
+
+function normalizeDescription(value: unknown): string | null {
+  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
+    return null;
+  }
+
+  if (typeof value !== "string") {
+    return null;
+  }
+
+  const trimmed = value.trim();
+  return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : null;
+}
+
+function normalizeOptionalFolderId(value: unknown, userId: number): { value?: number | null; error?: string } {
+  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
+    return { value: null };
+  }
+
+  if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isInteger(value) || value <= 0) {
+    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
+  }
+
+  const folder = getFolderById.get(value, userId);
+  if (!folder) {
+    return { error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" };
+  }
+
+  return { value };
+}
+
+function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
+  try {
+    new URL(value);
+    return true;
+  } catch {
+    return false;
+  }
+}
+
+function normalizeEmail(value: unknown): string | null {
+  if (typeof value !== "string") {
+    return null;
+  }
+
+  const email = value.trim().toLowerCase();
+  return /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email) ? email : null;
+}
+
+function normalizePassword(value: unknown): string | null {
+  return typeof value === "string" && value.length >= 8 ? value : null;
+}
+
+function validateAuthBody(body: AuthBody): { value?: { email: string; password: string }; error?: string } {
+  const email = normalizeEmail(body.email);
+  if (!email) {
+    return { error: "email must be a valid email address" };
+  }
+
+  const password = normalizePassword(body.password);
+  if (!password) {
+    return { error: "password must be at least 8 characters" };
+  }
+
+  return { value: { email, password } };
+}
+
+function toPublicUser(user: User): PublicUser {
+  return {
+    id: user.id,
+    email: user.email,
+    created_at: user.created_at,
+  };
+}
+
+function createToken(userId: number): string {
+  return jwt.sign({ userId }, JWT_SECRET);
+}
+
+function getAuthenticatedUserId(req: Request): number {
+  return (req as unknown as AuthenticatedRequest).userId;
+}
+
+function authenticate(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction): void {
+  const authorization = req.header("Authorization");
+  if (!authorization?.startsWith("Bearer ")) {
+    res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
+    return;
+  }
+
+  const token = authorization.slice("Bearer ".length).trim();
+  try {
+    const payload = jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET);
+    if (typeof payload !== "object" || typeof payload.userId !== "number") {
+      res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
+      return;
+    }
+
+    const user = getUserById.get(payload.userId);
+    if (!user) {
+      res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
+      return;
+    }
+
+    (req as AuthenticatedRequest).userId = payload.userId;
+    next();
+  } catch {
+    res.status(401).json({ error: "unauthorized" });
+  }
+}
+
+function validateCreateBookmark(
+  body: BookmarkBody,
+  userId: number,
+): { value?: Omit<Bookmark, "id" | "created_at" | "updated_at">; error?: string } {
+  const url = normalizeRequiredString(body.url);
+  if (!url) {
+    return { error: "url must be a non-empty string" };
+  }
+
+  if (!isValidUrl(url)) {
+    return { error: "url must be a valid URL" };
+  }
+
+  const title = normalizeRequiredString(body.title);
+  if (!title) {
+    return { error: "title must be a non-empty string" };
+  }
+
+  const folderId = normalizeOptionalFolderId(body.folder_id, userId);
+  if (folderId.error || folderId.value === undefined) {
+    return { error: folderId.error };
+  }
+
+  return {
+    value: {
+      url,
+      title,
+      description: normalizeDescription(body.description),
+      folder_id: folderId.value,
+    },
+  };
+}
+
+function validateFolderName(value: unknown): { value?: string; error?: string } {
+  const name = normalizeRequiredString(value);
+  if (!name) {
+    return { error: "name must be a non-empty string" };
+  }
+
+  return { value: name };
+}

+app.post("/auth/register", (req, res) => {

+app.use(["/bookmarks", "/folders"], authenticate);

+app.post("/bookmarks", (req, res) => {

+app.get("/bookmarks", (req, res) => {
+  const pagination = parseBookmarkPagination(req.query);
+  if (pagination.error || !pagination.value) {

+  res.status(200).json({

+    limit,
+    total: countBookmarks.get(userId)?.total ?? 0,

+
+app.get("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
+  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);

+  res.status(200).json(bookmark);

+app.put("/bookmarks/:id", (req, res) => {
+  const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
+  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
+  if (!id) {
+    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
+    return;
+  }
+
+  const existing = getBookmarkById.get(id, userId);
+  if (!existing) {
+    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
+    return;

+
+  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
+  const url = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : normalizeRequiredString(body.url);
+  if (!url) {
+    res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be a non-empty string" });

+
+  if (!isValidUrl(url)) {

+  const title = body.title === undefined ? existing.title : normalizeRequiredString(body.title);

+  const description = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(body, "description")

     2	
     3	Bookmarks are saved URLs with a title, optional description, optional folder, and creation/update timestamps. Folders are named collections of bookmarks. Users own their bookmarks and folders.
     4	
     5	## API Contract
     6	
     7	- `POST /auth/register` creates a user from valid `email` and `password`, stores a password hash, and returns the user without `password_hash`.
     8	- `POST /auth/login` authenticates a user from valid `email` and `password` and returns a JWT token.
     9	- All `/bookmarks` and `/folders` endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return `401` when the token is missing or invalid.
    10	- `POST /bookmarks` creates an authenticated user's bookmark from `url`, `title`, optional `description`, and optional `folder_id`.
    11	- `GET /bookmarks` returns the authenticated user's bookmarks in a paginated JSON envelope: `{ "data": [...], "page": 1, "limit": 20, "total": 45 }`.
    12	- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one owned bookmark or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
    13	- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates an owned bookmark, including optional `folder_id`, and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
    14	- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` removes an owned bookmark and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
    15	- `POST /folders` creates an authenticated user's folder from required `name` and returns `201`.
    16	- `GET /folders` returns the authenticated user's folders as a JSON array.
    17	- `GET /folders/:id` returns one owned folder with a `bookmarks` array or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
    18	- `PUT /folders/:id` updates an owned folder name and returns it, or `404` when it does not exist for that user.
    19	- `DELETE /folders/:id` removes an owned folder and returns `204`, or `404` when it does not exist for that user. Owned bookmarks in the deleted folder are not deleted; their `folder_id` becomes `null`.
    20	
    21	## Authentication
    22	
    23	- Register and login require a valid email address and a password of at least 8 characters.
    24	- Duplicate registration returns `409`.
    25	- Invalid login credentials return `401`.
    26	- JWT payloads include `userId`.
    27	- Authenticated bookmark and folder responses do not include password hashes.
    28	
    29	## Data Contract
    30	
    31	Bookmark records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `bookmarks` table:
    32	
    33	- `id`: integer primary key
    34	- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`
    35	- `url`: required text
    36	- `title`: required text
    37	- `description`: nullable text
    38	- `folder_id`: nullable integer foreign key to `folders.id`
    39	- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text
    40	- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text
    41	
    42	Folder records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `folders` table:
    43	
    44	- `id`: integer primary key
    45	- `user_id`: required integer foreign key to `users.id`
    46	- `name`: required text
    47	- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text
    48	- `updated_at`: ISO timestamp text
    49	
    50	User records are stored in SQLite `data.db` in the `users` table:
    51	
    52	- `id`: integer primary key
    53	- `email`: required unique text
    54	- `password_hash`: required text
    55	- `created_at`: ISO timestamp text
    56	
    57	## Validation
    58	
    59	- `url` is required for creation and must be a valid URL.
    60	- `url`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty valid URL.
    61	- `title` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
    62	- `title`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
    63	- Empty or whitespace-only titles and URLs are rejected with `400`.
    64	- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference an existing folder or the request returns `400`.
    65	- `folder_id`, when provided, must reference a folder owned by the authenticated user.
    66	- Folder `name` is required for creation and must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
    67	- Folder `name`, when provided on update, must be a non-empty string after trimming whitespace.
    68	- `email` is required for registration and login and must be a valid email address.
    69	- `password` is required for registration and login and must be at least 8 characters.
    70	
    71	## Pagination
    72	
    73	- `GET /bookmarks` accepts optional `page` and `limit` query parameters.
    74	- `page` defaults to `1` and must be an integer of at least `1`.
    75	- `limit` defaults to `20` and must be an integer from `1` through `100`.
    76	- Invalid `page` or `limit` values return `400`.
    77	- `total` is the authenticated user's full bookmark count before pagination.
    78	- `GET /folders/:id` continues to return its folder bookmarks as an unpaginated array.

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

No application stack is selected yet.

No application code exists yet. This document defines generic architecture
questions and boundary rules that future implementation should adapt after a
user-provided spec and stack decision exist.

## Discovery Before Shape

Before proposing implementation shape, identify:

- Product surfaces: browser, mobile, desktop, CLI, API, worker, or service.
- Runtime stack: language, framework, database, queues, providers, and hosting.
- Core domains: the product concepts that deserve stable names and contracts.
- Boundary inputs: user input, API requests, webhooks, jobs, files, credentials,
  provider payloads, and environment configuration.
- Validation ladder: the smallest checks that can prove the selected stack.

Record stack choices in `docs/decisions/` when they meaningfully constrain
future work.

## Default Layering

```text
domain
  <- application
      <- infrastructure
          <- interface
              <- app surfaces
```

## Candidate Structure

```text
app/
  domain/
    entities/
    value-objects/
    repositories/
    services/

  application/
    commands/
    queries/
    handlers/

  infrastructure/
    database/
    logging/
    notifications/

  interface/
    controllers/
    dto/
    presenters/
    routes/
    middlewares/

surfaces/
  browser/
  mobile/
  desktop/
  cli/
```

This is a thinking template, not a scaffold. Create real folders only when a
story enters implementation and the selected stack needs them.

## Dependency Rule

Inner layers must not depend on outer layers.

| Layer | May depend on | Must not depend on |
| --- | --- | --- |
| domain | nothing project-external except tiny pure utilities | framework, database, UI, provider, process/env |
| application | domain | framework, UI, provider, database concrete clients |
| infrastructure | domain, application | interface controllers or UI |
| interface | all backend layers | UI state or platform shell assumptions |
| app surfaces | API contracts and app-facing clients | domain internals directly |

## Parse-First Boundary Rule

Unknown data must be parsed at boundaries before it enters inner code.

Boundaries include:

- HTTP request bodies, params, and query strings.
- Session payloads and identity claims.
- Environment variables.
- Database rows returned from external clients.
- Platform shell payloads.
- Deep links, tokens, and signed URLs.
- Provider webhooks, events, and async payloads.

Target flow:

```text
unknown input
  -> parser
  -> typed DTO or command
  -> application use case
  -> domain object/value object
```

Inner layers should work with meaningful product types such as `UserId`,
`AccountId`, `WorkspaceId`, `Role`, `DateRange`, or domain-specific IDs,
rather than repeatedly validating raw strings.

## Command/Query Boundary

If the product has both reads and writes, keep command/query separation clear at
the code level even when the storage layer is simple:

- Commands mutate state and own audit side effects.
- Queries read state and format for consumers.
- Shared domain rules live in domain/application, not controllers.

## Observability Contract

The future server should emit one canonical JSON log line per request with:

- timestamp
- level
- request_id
- user_id when known
- action
- duration_ms

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:30:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_14","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh"","aggregated_output":"#!/usr/bin/env bash
# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl

check_functional() {
  local task="$1"
  local outdir="$2"
  local project_dir="$3"

  local results=()
  local server_pid=""
  local port=3000
  local base_url="http://localhost:$port"

  # Try to start the server
  (cd "$project_dir" && npm run dev > "$outdir/server.log" 2>&1) &
  server_pid=$!
  sleep 3

  # Check if server is alive
  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  Server failed to start"
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 10s)
  local ready=false
  for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$base_url/health" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|404\|500"; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done

  if [ "$ready" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  Server not responding on port $port"
    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Run task-specific checks
  case "$task" in
    T1-project-setup)
      run_check results "health_status" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      run_check results "health_body" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200 --body-contains '"status"'
      ;;

    T2-crud-bookmarks)
      # Create
      run_check results "create_bookmark" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test Bookmark"}'
      # List
      run_check results "list_bookmarks" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      # Get single
      run_check results "get_bookmark" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200
      # Update
      run_check results "update_bookmark" "PUT" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200 \
        --data '{"title":"Updated Title"}'
      # Delete
      run_check results "delete_bookmark" "DELETE" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 204
      # Validation
      run_check results "missing_title" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
      run_check results "missing_url" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"title":"No URL"}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "health_regression" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      ;;

    T3-folder-support)
      # Folder CRUD
      run_check results "create_folder" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 201 \
        --data '{"name":"Reading List"}'
      run_check results "list_folders" "GET" "$base_url/folders" 200
      run_check results "get_folder" "GET" "$base_url/folders/1" 200
      # Bookmark with folder
      run_check results "bookmark_with_folder" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Foldered","folder_id":1}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "bookmark_regression" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      ;;

    T4-authentication)
      # Register
      run_check results "register" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 201 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Duplicate
      run_check results "duplicate_email" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 409 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      run_check results "login" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 200 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Wrong password
      run_check results "wrong_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 401 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"wrongpass"}'
      # Protected (no auth)
      run_check results "protected_no_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      # Protected (with auth)
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "protected_with_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        # Create with auth
        run_check results "create_with_auth" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Authed Bookmark"}'
      fi
      ;;

    T5-bug-fix)
      # Login first
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      # If no existing user, register first
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_title_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":""}'
        run_check results "empty_url_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"","title":"Test"}'
        run_check results "valid_still_works" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Valid"}'
      fi
      # Folder empty name
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_folder_name" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"name":""}'
      fi
      ;;

    T6-pagination)
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        # Check paginated response shape
        local response
        response=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" 2>/dev/null)

        # Has expected fields
        run_check_json results "has_data_field" "$response" '.data'
        run_check_json results "has_page_field" "$response" '.page'
        run_check_json results "has_limit_field" "$response" '.limit'
        run_check_json results "has_total_field" "$response" '.total'
        run_check_json results "data_is_array" "$response" '.data | type == "array"'

        # Custom pagination
        run_check results "custom_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Invalid
        run_check results "invalid_page" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=0" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        run_check results "invalid_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Auth still required
        run_check results "still_protected" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # Stop server
  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true

  # Write results
  write_functional_json "$outdir/functional.json" "${results[@]}"
}

# Helper: run a single endpoint check
run_check() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local method="$1"; shift
  local url="$1"; shift
  local expected_status="$1"; shift

  local extra_args=()
  local body_contains=""
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --header) extra_args+=(-H "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --body-contains) body_contains="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      *) shift ;;
    esac
  done

  local response_code response_body
  response_body=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X "$method" "${extra_args[@]}" "$url" 2>/dev/null)
  response_code=$(echo "$response_body" | tail -1)
  response_body=$(echo "$response_body" | sed '$d')

  local pass=false
  if [ "$response_code" = "$expected_status" ]; then
    pass=true
  fi

  # Optional body check
  if [ -n "$body_contains" ] && [ "$pass" = "true" ]; then
    if ! echo "$response_body" | grep -q "$body_contains"; then
      pass=false
    fi
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass,\"expected\":$expected_status,\"actual\":$response_code}")
}

# Helper: check JSON response field
run_check_json() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local json="$1"; shift
  local jq_expr="$1"; shift

  local pass=false
  if echo "$json" | jq -e "$jq_expr" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    pass=true
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass}")
}

# Helper: write results JSON
write_functional_json() {
  local outfile="$1"; shift
  local checks=("$@")

  echo -n '{"server_started":true,"checks":[' > "$outfile"

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' docs/ARCHITECTURE.md"","aggregated_output":"# Architecture

No application stack is selected yet.

No application code exists yet. This document defines generic architecture
questions and boundary rules that future implementation should adapt after a
user-provided spec and stack decision exist.

## Discovery Before Shape

Before proposing implementation shape, identify:

- Product surfaces: browser, mobile, desktop, CLI, API, worker, or service.
- Runtime stack: language, framework, database, queues, providers, and hosting.
- Core domains: the product concepts that deserve stable names and contracts.
- Boundary inputs: user input, API requests, webhooks, jobs, files, credentials,
  provider payloads, and environment configuration.
- Validation ladder: the smallest checks that can prove the selected stack.

Record stack choices in `docs/decisions/` when they meaningfully constrain
future work.

## Default Layering

```text
domain
  <- application
      <- infrastructure
          <- interface
              <- app surfaces
```

## Candidate Structure

```text
app/
  domain/
    entities/
    value-objects/
    repositories/
    services/

  application/
    commands/
    queries/
    handlers/

  infrastructure/
    database/
    logging/
    notifications/

  interface/
    controllers/
    dto/
    presenters/
    routes/
    middlewares/

surfaces/
  browser/
  mobile/
  desktop/
  cli/
```

This is a thinking template, not a scaffold. Create real folders only when a
story enters implementation and the selected stack needs them.

## Dependency Rule

Inner layers must not depend on outer layers.

| Layer | May depend on | Must not depend on |
| --- | --- | --- |
| domain | nothing project-external except tiny pure utilities | framework, database, UI, provider, process/env |
| application | domain | framework, UI, provider, database concrete clients |
| infrastructure | domain, application | interface controllers or UI |
| interface | all backend layers | UI state or platform shell assumptions |
| app surfaces | API contracts and app-facing clients | domain internals directly |

## Parse-First Boundary Rule

Unknown data must be parsed at boundaries before it enters inner code.

Boundaries include:

- HTTP request bodies, params, and query strings.
- Session payloads and identity claims.
- Environment variables.
- Database rows returned from external clients.
- Platform shell payloads.
- Deep links, tokens, and signed URLs.
- Provider webhooks, events, and async payloads.

Target flow:

```text
unknown input
  -> parser
  -> typed DTO or command
  -> application use case
  -> domain object/value object
```

Inner layers should work with meaningful product types such as `UserId`,
`AccountId`, `WorkspaceId`, `Role`, `DateRange`, or domain-specific IDs,
rather than repeatedly validating raw strings.

## Command/Query Boundary

If the product has both reads and writes, keep command/query separation clear at
the code level even when the storage layer is simple:

- Commands mutate state and own audit side effects.
- Queries read state and format for consumers.
- Shared domain rules live in domain/application, not controllers.

## Observability Contract

The future server should emit one canonical JSON log line per request with:

- timestamp
- level
- request_id
- user_id when known
- action
- duration_ms
- status_code
- message

Audit logs are product records. Application logs are operational records. Do not
use one as a substitute for the other.

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:13:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

./benchmark/runs/baseline-main-valid-node22-20260525/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

import type { Request, Response } from "express";
import type Database from "better-sqlite3";
import { getAuthenticatedUserId } from "./auth";

type BookmarkRow = {
  id: number;
  user_id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkResponse = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

const toBookmarkResponse = (row: BookmarkRow): BookmarkResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  url: row.url,
  title: row.title,
  description: row.description,
  folder_id: row.folder_id,
  created_at: row.created_at,
  updated_at: row.updated_at,
});

const getBookmarkId = (req: Request, res: Response): number | null => {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);

  if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id < 1) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    return null;
  }

  return id;
};

const readString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value.trim();
};

const readOptionalFolderId = (value: unknown): number | null | undefined => {
  if (value === undefined || value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value !== "number" || !Number.isInteger(value) || value < 1) {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value;
};

export const createBookmarksRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();

  const findById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");
  const findFolderById = db.prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");

  const validateFolderId = (
    value: unknown,
    userId: number,
    res: Response,
  ): number | null | undefined => {
    const folderId = readOptionalFolderId(value);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return undefined;
    }

    if (folderId !== null && !findFolderById.get(folderId, userId)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return undefined;
    }

    return folderId;
  };

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const url = readString(req.body?.url);
    const title = readString(req.body?.title);
    const description = req.body?.description;
    const folderId = validateFolderId(req.body?.folder_id, userId, res);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      return;
    }

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO bookmarks (user_id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(
        userId,
        url,
        title,
        typeof description === "string" ? description : null,
        folderId,
        now,
        now,
      );

    const bookmark = findById.get(result.lastInsertRowid, userId) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(201).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.get("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const bookmarks = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all(userId) as BookmarkRow[];

    return res.status(200).json(bookmarks.map(toBookmarkResponse));
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const bookmark = findById.get(id, userId) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!bookmark) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(bookmark));
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findById.get(id, userId) as BookmarkRow | undefined;
    if (!existing) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    const url = req.body?.url === undefined ? existing.url : readString(req.body.url);
    const title =
      req.body?.title === undefined ? existing.title : readString(req.body.title);
    const description =
      req.body?.description === undefined
        ? existing.description
        : typeof req.body.description === "string"
          ? req.body.description
          : null;
    const folderId =
      req.body?.folder_id === undefined
        ? existing.folder_id
        : validateFolderId(req.body.folder_id, userId, res);

    if (folderId === undefined) {
      return;
    }

    if (!url) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be non-empty" });
    }

    if (!title) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    db.prepare(
      `
        UPDATE bookmarks
        SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
        WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
      `,
    ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id, userId);

    const updated = findById.get(id, userId) as BookmarkRow;

    return res.status(200).json(toBookmarkResponse(updated));
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getBookmarkId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      id,
      userId,
    );
    if (result.changes === 0) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Bookmark not found" });
    }

    return res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
};

};

  id: row.id,

});

});

const readString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {

  }

  return value.trim();
};

const getFolderId = (req: Request, res: Response): number | null => {
  const id = Number(req.params.id);

};

export const createFoldersRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();

  const findById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?");
  const findBookmarksByFolderId = db.prepare(
    "SELECT * FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC",
  );

  router.post("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const name = readString(req.body?.name);

    if (!name) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO folders (user_id, name, created_at, updated_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(userId, name, now, now);

    const folder = findById.get(result.lastInsertRowid, userId) as FolderRow;

    return res.status(201).json(toFolderResponse(folder));
  });

  router.get("/", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const folders = db
      .prepare("SELECT * FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC")
      .all(userId) as FolderRow[];

    return res.status(200).json(folders.map(toFolderResponse));
  });

  router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const folder = findById.get(id, userId) as FolderRow | undefined;
    if (!folder) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    const bookmarks = findBookmarksByFolderId.all(id, userId) as BookmarkRow[];
    const response: FolderWithBookmarksResponse = {
      ...toFolderResponse(folder),
      bookmarks: bookmarks.map(toBookmarkResponse),
    };

    return res.status(200).json(response);
  });

  router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findById.get(id, userId) as FolderRow | undefined;
    if (!existing) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    const name = readString(req.body?.name);
    if (!name) {
      return res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    }

    const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    db.prepare("UPDATE folders SET name = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      name,
      updatedAt,
      id,
      userId,
    );

    const updated = findById.get(id, userId) as FolderRow;

    return res.status(200).json(toFolderResponse(updated));
  });

  router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
    const userId = getAuthenticatedUserId(req);
    const id = getFolderId(req, res);
    if (id === null) {
      return;
    }

    const result = db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      id,
      userId,
    );
    if (result.changes === 0) {
      return res.status(404).json({ error: "Folder not found" });
    }

    return res.status(204).send();
  });

  return router;
};

import { Router } from "express";
import type { NextFunction, Request, Response } from "express";
import jwt from "jsonwebtoken";
import type Database from "better-sqlite3";

const JWT_SECRET = "benchmark-secret";

type UserRow = {
  id: number;
  email: string;
  password_hash: string;
  created_at: string;
};

type UserResponse = {
  id: number;
  email: string;
  created_at: string;
};

type AuthTokenPayload = {
  userId: number;
};

type AuthenticatedUser = {
  userId: number;
};

export type AuthenticatedRequest = Request & {
  user: AuthenticatedUser;
};

const toUserResponse = (row: UserRow): UserResponse => ({
  id: row.id,
  email: row.email,
  created_at: row.created_at,
});

const readString = (value: unknown): string | undefined => {
  if (typeof value !== "string") {
    return undefined;
  }

  return value.trim();
};

const isValidEmail = (email: string): boolean =>
  /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(email);

const readCredentials = (
  req: Request,
  res: Response,
): { email: string; password: string } | null => {
  const email = readString(req.body?.email)?.toLowerCase();
  const password = readString(req.body?.password);

  if (!email || !isValidEmail(email)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "email must be valid" });
    return null;
  }

  if (!password || password.length < 8) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "password must be at least 8 characters" });
    return null;
  }

  return { email, password };
};

export const createAuthRouter = (db: Database.Database): Router => {
  const router = Router();
  const findUserByEmail = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?");
  const findUserById = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?");

  router.post("/register", (req, res) => {
    const credentials = readCredentials(req, res);
    if (!credentials) {
      return;
    }

    const existing = findUserByEmail.get(credentials.email) as UserRow | undefined;
    if (existing) {
      return res.status(409).json({ error: "email already exists" });
    }

    const now = new Date().toISOString();
    const passwordHash = bcrypt.hashSync(credentials.password, 10);
    const result = db
      .prepare(
        `
          INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, created_at)
          VALUES (?, ?, ?)
        `,
      )
      .run(credentials.email, passwordHash, now);

    const user = findUserById.get(result.lastInsertRowid) as UserRow;
    return res.status(201).json(toUserResponse(user));
  });

  router.post("/login", (req, res) => {
    const credentials = readCredentials(req, res);
    if (!credentials) {
      return;
    }

    const user = findUserByEmail.get(credentials.email) as UserRow | undefined;
    if (!user || !bcrypt.compareSync(credentials.password, user.password_hash)) {
      return res.status(401).json({ error: "invalid credentials" });
    }

    const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, JWT_SECRET);
    return res.status(200).json({ token });
  });

  return router;
};

export const requireAuth = (
  req: Request,
  res: Response,
  next: NextFunction,
): void => {
  const authorization = req.header("Authorization");
  const [scheme, token] = authorization?.split(" ") ?? [];

  if (scheme !== "Bearer" || !token) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "authentication required" });
    return;
  }

  try {
    const payload = jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET) as AuthTokenPayload;
    if (!Number.isInteger(payload.userId) || payload.userId < 1) {
      res.status(401).json({ error: "invalid token" });
      return;
    }

    (req as AuthenticatedRequest).user = { userId: payload.userId };
    next();
  } catch {
    res.status(401).json({ error: "invalid token" });
  }
};

export const getAuthenticatedUserId = (req: Request): number =>
  (req as AuthenticatedRequest).user.userId;

normal

## Product Contract

The API persists bookmarks in SQLite and exposes create, list, read, update,
and delete operations at `/bookmarks`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark with required `url` and `title`.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns all bookmarks.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns one bookmark or `404`.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates one bookmark or returns `404`.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes one bookmark with `204` or returns `404`.
- Missing or empty required bookmark fields return `400`.
- `GET /health` continues to return `200`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, delete bookmark rows.
- Queries: list bookmarks and read bookmark by id.
- API: Express routes under `/bookmarks`.
- Tables: `bookmarks` in `data.db`.
- Domain rules: `url` and `title` are required non-empty strings.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build |
| Integration | Curl CRUD endpoint checks |
| E2E | Benchmark functional check for T2 |
| Platform | Node.js process starts on port 3000 |
| Release | Not applicable |

## Harness Delta

No harness changes expected.

## Evidence

- `npm run build` passed.
- Benchmark functional check for `T2-crud-bookmarks` passed: create, list, get,
  update, delete, missing title, missing URL, and health regression.
- Manual check passed: `POST /bookmarks` with an empty title returns `400`.
docs/stories/US-003-folder-support.md
# US-003 Folder Support

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The API persists folders in SQLite, lets bookmarks optionally belong to one
folder, and removes folder membership without deleting bookmarks when a folder
is deleted.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`
- `docs/stories/US-002-crud-bookmarks.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /folders` creates a folder with required non-empty `name`.
- `GET /folders` returns all folders.
- `GET /folders/:id` returns one folder with its bookmarks or `404`.
- `PUT /folders/:id` updates the folder name or returns `404`.
- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes the folder with `204` or returns `404`.
- Bookmarks can be created and updated with nullable `folder_id`.
- Non-existent or invalid `folder_id` values return `400`.
- Deleting a folder nullifies `folder_id` on associated bookmarks.
- Existing bookmark CRUD endpoints continue to work.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, delete folder rows; create and update bookmark
  folder membership.
- Queries: list folders, read folder by id, and read folder bookmarks.
- API: Express routes under `/folders`; bookmark routes accept `folder_id`.
- Tables: `folders`; `bookmarks.folder_id` nullable foreign key.
- Domain rules: folder `name` is required; `folder_id` must be null or refer to
  an existing folder.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build |
| Integration | Curl folder CRUD, bookmark folder assignment, invalid folder validation, delete nullification |
| E2E | Benchmark functional check for T3 |
| Platform | Node.js process starts on port 3000 |
| Release | Not applicable |

## Harness Delta

No harness changes expected.

## Evidence

- `npm run build` passed.
- `npm test -- --passWithNoTests` passed with no test files.
- Benchmark functional check for `T3-folder-support` passed.
- Benchmark functional check for `T2-crud-bookmarks` passed as regression proof.
- Manual curl checks passed for folder CRUD, bookmark `folder_id`, invalid
  `folder_id`, delete nullification, and bookmark CRUD regression.
